source_jobs.title: Staff Software Engineer
source_jobs.description:
QuoteWell is building a modern commercial insurance brokerage by combining technology with deep domain expertise. A key part of our vision is an AI assistant powered by emerging LLM techniques that helps brokers anticipate and automate tasks, making their workflows faster, smarter, and more intuitive.

We’re looking for an experienced engineer who wants to shape not just our technology, but also our product direction and business outcomes.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Build impactful products: Own and deliver features in our all-TypeScript stack (React + Node).

 - Shape product direction: Partner with Product, Design, and Data to define problems, break them down into incremental solutions, and validate with users.

 - Leverage AI in practice: Deliver innovative AI-driven agentic solutions for our insurance experts, designed for a future MCP-driven world.

 - Model complex data: Represent insurance data and relationships in intuitive, user-friendly tools that improve decision-making.

 - Collaborate and grow: Work in a remote-first, flexible, and accountable engineering culture while learning from teammates (and maybe a bit about insurance too).


WHAT YOU’LL BRING

 - Technical depth: Strong experience with TypeScript, React, and Node (or a very similar stack), and a history of shipping production code.

 - Product impact: A track record of influencing product and business direction through engineering insights.

 - AI curiosity: Familiarity with or interest in applied AI/LLM techniques and how they can power real products.

 - Collaborative skills: Ability to work across the full product lifecycle—specifying features, testing, iterating, and validating with users.

 - Generalist mindset: Willingness to learn new technologies and adapt to what the business needs most.

 - Flexibility to connect in person: Willingness to travel occasionally to Austin or team offsites.

Location: Austin, TX or Hybrid Remote. Our primary hub is located in Austin, TX, and we have remote employees across the United States. We offer flexible co-working arrangements in locations with multiple engineers.

What we offer our full-time employees

 - Competitive salaries + equity in the company

 - Medical, Dental, and Vision healthcare benefits + FSA and HSA options

 - Flexible vacation policy to recharge and live life

 - Paid membership to One Medical for employees, spouses / domestic partners, and dependents

 - Company-sponsored 401K plan (Roth & Traditional)

 - Paid parental leave, generous family forming benefits and up to $5,000 of reimbursements through Carrot

 - Up to $1,000 in home office reimbursements to build a productive space to work remotely

 - An opportunity to work on solutions that are redefining the future of commercial insurance

 - A culture driven by a focus on impact, a shared growth mindset, empowered individuals, and integrity

source_jobs.title: Broker — Midwest Region
source_jobs.description:
The Role

QuoteWell is seeking an established broker to join our team as a Vice President or Senior Vice President and help shape the next phase of our growth. We’re looking for a dynamic leader who understands the industry’s challenges and is motivated to build smarter, more innovative solutions. With a flat structure and unlimited opportunity for advancement, QuoteWell offers the chance to grow a practice without traditional constraints.



What you’ll do

 - Drive sales and new carrier appointments to grow QuoteWell's footprint

 - Support the continued evolution of wholesale operations at QuoteWell

 - Develop, maintain and cultivate client and agent relationships.

 - Solicit, market, quote, bind, and confirm new and existing business.

 - Negotiate the best terms and coverages available with accessible markets.

 - Partner with our Product, Engineering, and Operations teams to build solutions to streamline efforts across the full policy lifecycle in the surplus lines space



What you’ll need

 - Sufficient experience that allowed you to build a sizable book of business.

 - Necessary licenses for your appropriate lines of business

 - Strong sales / producer abilities

 - Ability to handle high-volume deals, generating quotes and servicing policies quickly

 - Excellent communication skills: both in-person and written

 - Organized and detail oriented

 - A natural desire to see things through and a history of being scrappy when it counts

 - Willingness and ability to travel depending on the needs of clients, Agents, and new business



Location

This is a remote position. Preference will be given to candidates located in IL, MN, and OH, or those who currently support or manage accounts in this region.

What we offer our full-time employees

 - Competitive salaries + equity in the company

 - Medical, Dental, and Vision healthcare benefits + FSA and HSA options

 - Flexible vacation policy to recharge and live life

 - Paid membership to One Medical for employees, spouses / domestic partners, and dependents

 - Company-sponsored 401K plan (Roth & Traditional)

 - Paid parental leave, generous family forming benefits and up to $5,000 of reimbursements through Carrot

 - Up to $1,000 in home office reimbursements to build a productive space to work remotely

 - An opportunity to work on solutions that are redefining the future of commercial insurance

 - A culture driven by a focus on impact, a shared growth mindset, empowered individuals, and integrity

source_jobs.title: Senior Underwriter— West Region
source_jobs.description:
The Role

QuoteWell is seeking a commercial insurance underwriter to join our team and help shape the next phase of our growth. We’re looking for a dynamic leader who understands the industry’s challenges and is motivated to build smarter, more innovative solutions. With a flat structure and unlimited opportunity for advancement, QuoteWell offers the chance to grow a practice without traditional constraints.


What you’ll do

 - Drive growth. Bring in new business and expand our carrier footprint through smart, strategic appointments and strong producer relationships.

 - Own outcomes. Navigate the quote-bind-issue lifecycle from start to finish, ensuring fast, accurate turnaround on every deal.

 - Operate like an owner. Build and evolve QuoteWell’s wholesale capabilities, especially within binding authority lines, driving efficiency and scale as we grow.

 - Work across teams. Partner with Engineering, Product, and Operations to design and implement tools that make underwriting in surplus lines smoother and smarter.
   

What you’ll need

 - 3+ years of experience in a wholesale underwriting or production role, with a strong record of success in E&S.

 - Active home state general and surplus lines licenses

 - Producer mindset. You know how to sell, build relationships, and close deals.

 - Fast-paced execution. You thrive in a high-volume environment and can generate quotes and service policies at speed without sacrificing quality.

 - Clear communicator. You write crisply, speak clearly, and can adapt your tone to agents, carriers, and teammates.

 - Organized and accountable. You juggle multiple priorities, track every detail, and don’t drop the ball.

 - Problem-solver. When something breaks, you figure it out. When something’s missing, you build it.

 - Team-first attitude. You roll up your sleeves, help your teammates, and celebrate shared wins.

 - A valid driver’s license and openness to occasional travel.
   

Location

This is a remote position. Preference will be given to candidates located in CA, UT, AZ, and CO, or those who currently support or manage accounts in this region.

What we offer our full-time employees

 - Competitive salaries + equity in the company

 - Medical, Dental, and Vision healthcare benefits + FSA and HSA options

 - Flexible vacation policy to recharge and live life

 - Paid membership to One Medical for employees, spouses / domestic partners, and dependents

 - Company-sponsored 401K plan (Roth & Traditional)

 - Paid parental leave, generous family forming benefits and up to $5,000 of reimbursements through Carrot

 - Up to $1,000 in home office reimbursements to build a productive space to work remotely

 - An opportunity to work on solutions that are redefining the future of commercial insurance

 - A culture driven by a focus on impact, a shared growth mindset, empowered individuals, and integrity

source_jobs.title: Construction Underwriter (Programs)
source_jobs.description:
The Role

Range by Quotewell is Quotewell’s programs division, focused on building technology-enabled insurance solutions for complex risks. We are hiring a Construction Underwriter, with deep experience in project excess liability and contractors-related construction risks, to help support and scale our growing programs business.

This person will be a senior, hands-on underwriter who understands the industry’s challenges and is motivated to build smarter, more innovative solutions.

The ideal candidate has a strong E&S underwriting background (carrier or MGU) and is able to own the process from day one, evaluate complex construction submissions, and make strong underwriting decisions with speed, accuracy, and sound market judgment. They should combine deep technical coverage expertise with an entrepreneurial mindset and excitement for helping shape a high-growth program platform supported by technology and AI-enabled underwriting workflows.

What you’ll do

 - Drive growth. Build, manage, and grow strong relationships with wholesale brokers and agent partners to drive profitable business flow.

 - Own outcomes. Navigate the full underwriting lifecycle from start to finish, ensuring fast, accurate turnaround on every deal while using technology-enabled workflows and underwriting tools to improve efficiency, reduce noise, and accelerate decision-making.

 - Operate like an owner. Help scale Kinetic’s construction program from the ground up by establishing strong underwriting practices early and driving efficiency as the business line grows.

 - Shape the platform. Partner with Engineering, Product, and Operations to design and implement tools that make program underwriting in surplus lines smoother and smarter.

What you’ll need

 - 7+ years of underwriting experience in a carrier, MGA, or MGU and a strong record of success in E&S underwriting.

 - Construction expertise. Ample experience underwriting construction risks, including project excess liability, wrap-ups, and contractor business.

 - Comfort building relationships. Strong existing relationships across the wholesale distribution marketplace and a track record of building trusted agent and broker partnerships.

 - Technical competency. Deep technical coverage expertise and the ability to evaluate exclusions, limitations, endorsements, and pricing with confidence.

 - Active home state general and surplus lines licenses, preferred.

 - Fast-paced execution. Excel in high pressure and high-volume environments, generating quotes and servicing policies at speed without sacrificing quality.

 - Clear communicator. Write crisply, speak clearly, and can adapt tone between agents, carriers, and teammates.

 - Organized and accountable. Ability to multitask and remain attentive to detail while balancing multiple priorities. Not dropping the ball.

 - Independent operator. Exercise strong judgment, build trust quickly, and ramp up efficiently.

 - Problem-solver. When something breaks, you figure it out. When something’s missing, you build it.

 - Team-first attitude. You roll up your sleeves, help your teammates, and celebrate shared wins.

 - A valid driver’s license and openness to travel.

Location

This is a remote position.

What we offer our full-time employees

 - Competitive salaries + equity in the company

 - Medical, Dental, and Vision healthcare benefits + FSA and HSA options

 - Flexible vacation policy to recharge and live life

 - Paid membership to One Medical for employees, spouses / domestic partners, and dependents

 - Company-sponsored 401K plan (Roth & Traditional)

 - Paid parental leave, generous family forming benefits and up to $5,000 of reimbursements through Carrot

 - Up to $1,000 in home office reimbursements to build a productive space to work remotely

 - An opportunity to work on solutions that are redefining the future of commercial insurance

 - A culture driven by a focus on impact, a shared growth mindset, empowered individuals, and integrit

source_jobs.title: Applied AI Engineer
source_jobs.description:
About QuoteWell

We started as a vertical SaaS company in insurance. We grew into operating our own wholesale brokerage and a programs group. Through that, we've lived the operational reality of applying AI in insurance — what works in production, what breaks, what compounds. We've just closed fresh funding to scale this operating model to retail insurance agencies across the country.

Our CEO, Joey Bouchard, built and ran applied AI teams at Palantir. We bring that technical rigor together with years of hands-on insurance operations experience to help agencies deploy AI that works in the real world. (Why insurance is worth this attention. https://medium.com/@jbouchard_6468/why-insurance-is-awesome-e5a00daa752b)



Why this role exists

Brokerage execution runs on heroics. Service quality depends on who owns the account, how overloaded they are, and what they happen to remember at the right moment. A submission gets marketed broadly, or it doesn't. A renewal gets the same rigor as new business, or it doesn't. A missing detail gets chased, or it stalls.

We don't think that's inevitable. Brokerage execution can be made systematic: software, automation, and AI carrying disciplined follow-through alongside human experts, inside clear bounds, in a regulated environment.

The hard part isn't the model call. It's governability — making AI accountable enough to trust with real deal flow. Who is the AI acting for? What is it allowed to do? What happens when it's wrong? In a regulated industry, those aren't side questions. They're the engineering problem.

We've been answering them inside our own wholesale brokerage for years. That's where we test whether the model actually produces more reliable, scalable, and consistent service. If it doesn't work for us, it doesn't deserve to be packaged for the market. Now we're packaging it.



The role

Applied AI Engineers sit at the point where Terminal, our platform, meets the messy reality of a customer's agency. You own the outcome at the customer. You also own the signal back to the rest of the company.

A typical month: you're embedded at a retail agency, working alongside their operators. You're identifying the workflows where heroics break down — submissions, renewals, follow-up, the work that today depends on someone's memory — and you're building governed AI execution into those workflows on Terminal. You're calibrating where humans stay (judgment, relationships, accountability) and where the system carries the standard (follow-through, coordination, attention).

The hardest and most interesting part: making it trustworthy. How AI decisions get reviewed, attributed, and defended. That work is what determines whether any of this can actually live in production.

Then you bring it all back. Engineering, Product, and Design need to know what generalizes and what doesn't, so the platform compounds across every customer instead of fragmenting into one-offs. You're the conduit.



What you'll do

 - Embed at a retail agency and own customer-side execution end to end

 - Build governed AI workflows on Terminal — integrating with AMS, carrier portals, email, and the systems an agency actually runs on

 - Identify which parts of an agency's operation can be made system-governed without losing the judgment that has to stay with people

 - Design the review, attribution, and override patterns that let a regulated business trust the system

 - Carry field signal back to Engineering, Product, and Design so the platform scales rather than splinters

 - Represent QuoteWell with customers — scope, troubleshoot, and reason about problems out loud, in their conference room

 - Travel to customer sites ~25% of the time



What we're looking for

 - 1-5 years shipping production software (Python and/or TypeScript a plus)

 - Strong engineering foundation — CS degree typical, talented dropouts welcome

 - Hands-on experience with modern AI application patterns, or the aptitude and hunger to get there fast

notes: QuoteWell is a commercial insurance brokerage with a strong focus on technology and AI. The company has a mix of technical and non-technical roles, with a slight emphasis on technical positions.
